sources.notes: GOV.UK states that building regulations approval is different from planning permission and that both may be needed. It also states homeowners may not need to apply themselves if they use a registered competent person scheme member for covered work.
sources.use_in_site: Primary source for approval-needed pages, route checkers and planning/building-regs comparisons.

sources.notes: GOV.UK explains full plans, building notice and completion certificate expectations. It also separates ordinary work from higher-risk building work.
sources.use_in_site: Primary source for application route pages, certificate timing blocks and route comparison tools.

sources.notes: The collection explains Approved Documents as statutory guidance on ways to meet building regulations, not the regulations themselves.
sources.use_in_site: Primary source for all Approved Document hubs and source/version notices.

sources.notes: Published 24 March 2026. Supports Part L of Schedule 1 to the Building Regulations 2010. Earlier versions continue to apply to buildings subject to previous regulatory standards.
sources.use_in_site: Energy-efficiency pages, Part L warning banners, version-aware source blocks.

sources.notes: Circular Letter 01/2026 announces new statutory guidance in Approved Document L volumes 1 and 2 and Approved Document F volume 1.
sources.use_in_site: Source guardrail for 2026 energy/ventilation pages and release/update blocks.

sources.notes: BSR oversees and approves building work for higher-risk buildings. The source lists height/storey and use thresholds.
sources.use_in_site: HRB gating route, no domestic simplification when project triggers BSR path.

codex_rule: Every content page must carry a source block with source IDs from this snapshot or the current source registry. Any page touching Part L, Part F or HRB status must include a visible version/route warning.
